steps.title: Count the doubling periods
steps.content:
From $1700$ to $1820$ is:
$$1820-1700=120$$
Since the population doubled every $30$ years, this is:
$$\frac{120}{30}=4$$
doubling periods.

steps.title: Reverse the doublings
steps.content:
Four doublings multiply the original population by $2^4=16$. Let $P$ be the population in $1700$:
$$16P=272{,}000$$
$$P=17{,}000$$
The answer is A.
